summary: Pre-tracking baseline. Not one stable contract but a RANGE of evolving dogfood harness shapes: no project-state file and no release/migration ledger existed, so installed projects were untracked and drifted freely as the scaffold changed.
notes: Schema 1 is a reconciliation floor, not a conformance certificate: an old install adopting tracking (via /materia:migrate --apply) gains only .materia/project.json — it does NOT retrofit every later scaffold change. Known manual reconciliation items an old 0.1.0 install may still need, which init-project-state does not touch: (1) the check:docs gate script — it REPLACED scripts/check-docs.mjs and now lives canonically at .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh; as of schema 3 this item is superseded by the 0.3.0-check-docs-sh-gate and 0.3.0-scripts-relocation entries, which /materia:migrate --apply now installs/relocates automatically (via the install-check-docs migration, copying from scaffold/.materia/scripts/check-docs.sh and stamping schema 3) and whose manualMigration text covers the by-hand path incl. dropping the old .mjs; (2) MATERIA.md § Version control and § Forge sections; (3) the .materia/review-angles/ review-angle library; (4) MATERIA.md § Review angles and § Skill routing sections. Reconcile items (2)-(4) by hand against the current scaffold; schema currency alone certifies none of them.
